response: IT IS CRITICAL TO MAINTAIN GOOD ORAL HYGIENE AND TAKE PRECAUTIONARY MEASURES UNTIL YOU CAN SEE A DENTIST. KEEP THE AREA CLEAN BY BRUSHING GENTLY AND THOROUGHLY AT LEAST TWICE A DAY WITH A SOFT-BOISTLED TOOTHBRUSH. AFTER EVERY MEAL, RINSE YOUR MOUTH WITH WATER TO REMOVE FOOD PARTICLES THAT MAY GET LODGED IN THE CAVITY. FLOSS CAREFULLY AROUND THE AFFECTED TOOTH TO REMOVE FOOD PARTICLES, BUT BE GENTLE TO AVOID CAUSING MORE DAMAGE OR PAIN. AVOID HARD, STICKY, OR CHEWEY FOODS THAT COULD CAUSE FURTHER DAMAGE TO THE TOOTH OR GET STUCK IN THE CAVITY. IT IS WISE TO AVOID EXTREMES LIKE VERY HOT OR COLD FOODS AND BEVERAGES THAT MAY CAUSE PAIN OR SENSITIVITY. USE TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS SOLD IN PHARMACIES TO FILL THE VOID TEMPORARILY AND PROTECT THE TOOTH UNTIL YOU CAN SEE A DENTIST. FOLLOW THE INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY TO AVOID ANY HARM. PAIN MANAGEMENT CAN BE DONE WITH OVER-THE-COUNTER PAIN RELIEVERS LIKE IBUPROFEN OR ACETAMINOPHEN, BUT AVOID ASPIRIN WHICH CAN CAUSE BLEEDING IF APPLIED DIRECTLY TO THE GUMS OR THE CAVITY. SALIN WATER RINSE TO REDUCE INFLAMMATION AND BACTERIA. MIX ABOUT 1/2 TEASPOON OF SALT IN 8 OUNCES OF WARM WATER AND RINSE YOUR MOUTH GENTLY. AVOID CHWING ON THE OPPOSITE SIDE OF YOUR MOUTH TO AVOID PUTTING PRESSURE ON THE DAMAGED TOOTH. MONITOR FOR CHANGES OR SIGNS OF INFECTION, INCLUDING INCREASED PAIN, SENSITIVITY, SWELLING, OR A BAD TASTE IN THE MOUTH. SEEK DENTAL CARE IMMEDIATELY IF YOU DEVELOP SIGNS OF A DENTAL ABSCESS, SUCH AS SEVERE PAIN, SWELLING, FEVER, OR LYMPH NODE ENLARGEMENT UNDER THE JAW. REMEMBER THAT THESE MEASURES ARE ONLY TEMPORARY AND IT IS CRUC
